# My Whiteboard

> Local-first whiteboard for engineers built on the tldraw SDK, where coding
> agents are first-class users: the canvas is structured data, read and edited
> by code over a local API — never by screenshots.

Desktop app (Electron), single-user, portable `.mywb` files. Agents connect
through a localhost HTTP API, an MCP server (`mywb mcp`), or fully headless
through the `mywb file` CLI.

## Docs

- [README](README.md): what the product is, dev commands, agent API summary
- [Product positioning](docs/product-positioning-abstract.md): problem, moat,
  long-term hybrid architecture (Vietnamese)
- [System architecture](docs/system-architecture.md): monorepo layout, core vs
  adapters, agent surfaces
- [Codebase summary](docs/codebase-summary.md): package-by-package map
- [Roadmap](docs/project-roadmap.md): shipped stages and decisions

## Agent integration

- [CI drift-check example](examples/ci-drift-check/README.md): keep a `.mywb`
  architecture diagram honest against the code on every PR
- [Drift-check agent skill](examples/ci-drift-check/SKILL.md): shape
  semantics, drift procedure, scaffold/update patterns
- Runtime: a running app serves `GET /llms.txt` and `GET /readme` on its
  localhost agent API (port and bearer token in `server.json`)
- Mermaid bridge: `mywb file mermaid <board>` exports a board as Mermaid
  (flowchart or `--syntax c4`); to import a Mermaid diagram, translate it to
  a scaffold model JSON yourself and run `mywb file scaffold` — no parser
  needed

## Optional

- [Architecture board](docs/architecture.mywb): this repo's own diagram as a
  `.mywb` file — read it with `mywb file read docs/architecture.mywb --json`
